mnemonics: Hot Priority: Highest Input Gets the Output (HP-HIGO)
keyPoints: An encoder converts a one-hot input (a single active line) into a binary code representing its position.
A priority encoder resolves conflicts when multiple inputs are active by outputting the code for the highest-priority input.
The binary output of an encoder has fewer bits (log2(n)) than the number of input lines (n).
commonMistakes: Forgetting that a standard encoder requires inputs to be one-hot; multiple active inputs cause incorrect output.
Confusing the function of an encoder (many-to-few) with a decoder (few-to-many), which perform opposite operations.
Misinterpreting the output of a priority encoder when multiple inputs are active, assuming it's an error rather than defined behavior.
